vulnerability_detection.justification: All four vulnerabilities identified with precise attack-vector descriptions: reflected XSS via URL param in dangerouslySetInnerHTML (CRITICAL), stored XSS via bio field (HIGH), javascript:/data: URL injection in href (HIGH), and </script> breakout in inline script block (Medium). Severity classifications are correct and match expectations.

fix_correctness.justification: Every fix addresses the root cause correctly: dangerouslySetInnerHTML on h1 replaced with safe JSX interpolation, DOMPurify.sanitize() applied to bio, sanitizeUrl() uses URL parsing to allowlist only http/https protocols, and Unicode escape sequences (\u003c etc.) prevent </script> injection while keeping valid JSON. No fix is bypassable.

fix_completeness.justification: All four identified vulnerabilities have corresponding fixes applied in the code, and AUDIT.md documents each finding and its remediation. No finding is left as a TODO or note without a fix.

regression_avoidance.justification: All original functionality is preserved: h1 renders greeting and name, bio renders rich HTML (sanitised), website link is kept (with URL validation), avatar image renders, window.__USER__ is still populated, and loading state is unchanged.

framework_idiom.justification: Fixes are idiomatic React: JSX text interpolation used where appropriate, hooks unchanged, DOMPurify integrated at render time (not in state), URL validation extracted to a pure helper, and inline script escaping uses valid JS Unicode sequences. The code reads as though written by a React expert.

expectations_met: FIXES: Removes dangerouslySetInnerHTML from the h1 — replaces with JSX interpolation
FIXES: Sanitises bio with DOMPurify before passing to dangerouslySetInnerHTML
FIXES: Validates profile.website URL protocol, rejects javascript: and data: schemes
FIXES: Escapes </script> sequences in inline JSON using Unicode escape sequences
PRESERVES: Component still renders greeting, name, bio, website link, avatar, and serialised profile data
DOES NOT: Flag img src={profile.avatar} as an XSS vector
DOES NOT: Flag {profile.name} in the alt attribute
DOES NOT: Remove the website link — preserved with URL validation
AUDIT: Correctly classifies greeting XSS as CRITICAL
AUDIT: Correctly classifies bio XSS as HIGH
AUDIT: Mentions the javascript: URL risk for the href
additional_observations: The AUDIT.md note on the script-injection fix references HTML entities in prose (& < >) but the actual code correctly uses JavaScript Unicode escape sequences (\u0026 \u003c \u003e) — this is a markdown rendering artefact in the documentation, not an error in the fix.
The AUDIT.md includes a dependency note advising use of isomorphic-dompurify for SSR environments, which is a useful production concern not required by the eval.
